review_text:
Beautiful location and pet friendly.

Right in the lake and great amenities and kitchen 😍😍😍

I stayed for a week at NRMA Jindabyne and found staff really helpful and friendly. The location near shops was convenient and the quality of my Studio room was excellent. The room is small but for only me it was perfect. Would also be good for a couple if they did not have much luggage (I had lots). As weather was warm for the time of year I thoroughly enjoyed sitting outside under the front verandah at end of day. I was sad to leave at the end of my stay and would definitely stay again in future.
My mate found a pair of crocks
Great like Park awesome staff close to everything you need
Beautiful location with clean amenities and friendly staff. My only feedback is the sites are too small for today's bigger vans. You can fit them in, but very little room for anything else.

Very nice location, just opposite the lake. Easy to take a morning walk along the river. The carbin is well equipped, the water is hot and stable, there is the air conditioner as well to keep night warm in the winter.

We had a wonderful stay at this caravan park and would highly recommend it for families with young children.

The location is beautiful, especially with the peaceful river nearby. It’s perfect for kids to explore, play, and enjoy nature safely. The environment feels calm, clean, and very family-friendly.

Our caravan was comfortable and well-maintained, with everything we needed for a relaxing stay. The area was quiet at night, which made it easy for our little one to sleep well.

Overall, it’s a great place to unwind, spend quality time with family, and let kids enjoy the outdoors. We would definitely come back again!

Visited and booked a powered site for the Easter long weekend. Site was just enough for a car, a gazebo and a tent. The site already had a mat for the tent so it made it super easy to keep the tent bottom clean.

Ample amenities (included study room and game room) and very clean showers (provided clean bathmats which was really appreciated). Made use of the pizza oven which was a delightful surprise - woolies isn't too far away.

Plenty of Easter activities for the kids and within walking distance along the lake to the sculptures by the lake for Easter. What a perfect location!

Staff were a 10/10 as well. Honestly the best site I've stayed at! Would definitely be back the next time I visit the snowy mountains.
Great spot in a top location around Jindabyne. The park is well set out and close to everything you need. Clean campsites and clean amenities, Looking forward to going back. 👍🏽

Clean amenities.
Great location.
Great central location

A great campsite right on Lake Jindabyne.  We went in a campervan and were pitched up right on the lake which was really nice!  Great views and walks around the lake.  Amenities were clean, the bigger block of toilets with showers was nicer than the smaller block but both do the job!  Be prepared for lots of mosquitos due to the lakeside location, but if you use repellent and cover up at night you’ll be okay.  A great stay!

Very good well-located holiday park. Telephone staff were really helpful when booking our caravan site. The site itself was well located overlooking the lake, near shops and walking path. Howver the site was narrow and shallow making it difficult even for my experienced husband to reverse the van into and requiring a jack to lift front. Amenities are well maintained, there is a separate shower + toilets & toilet blocks, camp kitchen, reading room, games room, herb garden.

The cabin's cleanliness was downright unacceptable upon checkin. Dust and cobweb build-up on and around the AC unit, on the cabinetry in the kitchen, and covering the TV remote. Along with hair from previous visitors on the bathroom baseboard and leaves/dirt right beside the couch at the entrance. The quiet hours here were more of a suggestion as slamming doors, horns honking, and roaring of truck engines started at 5am on a Saturday.

I would not recommend staying here and will be taking my business elsewhere in the future.
Really beautiful stay during our motorbike trip!
Fantastic country town 👏 👌
Had a brumby room for 3 nights was excellent...very clean showers and toilets..will be back

This  park in a great spot & staff are super helpful and friendly. site are very average though given the the perfect aspect to the lake & town.  needs work on managing water and site levels. would stay again though as a great location! Thanks to the team here 👍

Wir hatten den Glamtainer gebucht in der Meinung, dass es auch ein etwas gehobenerer Standard sei. Nunja, die Inneneinrichtung ist gut und die Aussicht auch.
Leider hat es keine Sonnenstore oder etwas ähnliches, die Terrasse liegt knall an der Sonne, somit erst kurz vor Sonnenuntergang brauchbar. Es hat auch keine Gartenstühle, damit man sich hinter dem Glamtainer im Schatten hinsetzen könnte.
Der Tisch im Innern wackelte weil der Fuss mit dem Sockel nicht mehr richtig verbunden ist. Wir haben dies gemeldet, jedoch nichts ist während unserem Aufenthalt passiert.
Ein Highlight auf der Terrasse ist sicher der Weber-Grill. Leider musten wir ihn am ersten Abend reinigen und erst noch den Brenner reinigen, damit er gestartet werden konnte. Immerhin wurde er am nächsten Morgen im üblichen Reinigungszyklus gereinigt.
Der Glamtainer weist zwar verspiegelte Fenster auf, nur sind diese Wärmedurchlässig, d.h. ein Kampf Klimaanlage -Sonne wird ausgefochten, welcher ohne gezogene Vorhänge zugusten der Sonne ausgeht.
Und wer den Glamtainer mietet, damit er oder sie einen Vloggingbeitrag veröffentlichen kann, dem sei viel Geduld mit der Wifi-Verbindung gewünscht.
Fazit: Der Park am See ok, Glamtainer nur wer Geld verschwenden will.

Great clean amenities, unbeatable location, perfect for exploring Jindabyne. If you’re exploring the alps and you want a place to stay in town, a hot shower, and a tidy kitchen, this is the spot. Right on the lake too, which was wonderful. Staff were also very friendly.
First class facilities and first class location

Don't believe you will see lake or mountains. As they advertise.  Not sure  We had a booking for 9 days. Put us on site 47. But not only we were discussed 4 other vans felt the same. On the  road to the entrance, looking at bins and back of an unkeep house. |Also we asked about other sites no go. But vans where moving in and out the whole time. They should have place longer stayers in the actually CP not out in the street entry. Also the lady who booked us in was dismissive and really should not be on a counter. Told me I wasn't booked in after finding my confirmed text she just said Oh... the before  me had a similar experience.|Very disappointed. It ruined our stay.
A beautiful location at Jindy! We've had a great time here. The provided bath mats in bathrooms is a win, especially with kids, makes shower time easier. Thank you!
Great location, sites right on the water, amenities are nice and clean. Would be good to see just dirt sites remediated and not available to be booked. We used one as just parking.
Our cabin was very small only 2 of us but no shelf space in bathroom no couch in main room beds were comfortable and the best shower water wise so strong lovely host

Edit 13/02/26:
Unfortunately, NRMA Jindabyne have proved my point with their reply below. Very disappointing after the situation I explained in my review - avoid this holiday park!

Review:
Although I enjoyed my stay, I’m giving 1 star because of the unfriendly and rude women on reception.

I booked through my partner, however one of the receptionists shouted at me in front of the bathroom because she did not believe I was staying at the holiday park. Even after I showed proof of my booking, she continued yelling at me in front of other guests. This made me feel very uncomfortable and we left earlier than planned.

I have also made a formal complaint via the Big4 website.

Make sure you are not on a site near the main road through town. Trucks roaring along using engine brakes 6am and onwards - you won’t be sleeping in! Other than this the place is very good in an excellent location close to shops and restaurants. It is surrounded by beauty but insist on getting a spot down by the lake for a peaceful stay.
Thank you we had a lovely stay. Jindabyne is beautiful. Very friendly staff. Spotlessly clean ammenities. Perfect spot on the lake. Walk to shops.

I don’t recommend this holiday park for solo female travellers. I emailed the staff during my first night about the fluorescent light above my campsite and how my whole tent illuminated. I find it invasive and it made me uncomfortable how bright the light was. I don’t like that anyone could just walk past and see into my tent, it’s an invasion of privacy. The following morning, I got an email from Julie with no attempt to make accomodations such as moving me to a different site (of which many were available). Just a “sorry, we are not able to turn off the park lights”.

On my drive back to Sydney, I had to pull into Cooma maccas for a nap. I had a better sleep in my car in a maccas parking lot than I did at NRMA Jindabyne.

One extra star because of the location. Easy access to swim in Lake Jindabyne, close to Jindabyne city centre, close to Kosciusko National Park.

Won’t be staying here for when I return to Jindabyne. I’ve stayed many times at NRMA Bright in Victoria. Thought I would like this one too. Such a shame.

This place was the best key word (was) me and my family owned a permanent caravan. That caravan was our whole life we had so many great memories there until they decided to kick us all out the owners of this place are rich business people that have 0 care in the world for average people like myself and others, there acts throughout this process was disgusting I would never recommend anyone stay here your supporting a soulless company that has 0 care for you except your money
Awesome park, definitely would love to come back. Beautiful place to swim, SUPA, cycle and go for walks.
Think again if you bring your van here. Cramped inverted site. Someone designed caravan site without understand how caravan works it seems!

Just had a extremely pleasant phone call with Julie from reception to confirm a booking
It was so easy and the reception team truely go out of their way to ensure you are looked after
It’s why I keep going back

Thanks Ladies

Bathmats!! Fresh for you to use in the communal showers
Great location, friendly staff and all the other good things people have said….. but bath mats- clean fresh, so good

Great stay, beautiful staff, well maintained facilities, sites were great, our cabin was great, Naomi made sure we all had a fantastic time with kids activities, movies and live music. Can't wait to go back!!!

This place was awesome as it gave a great experience exploring Lake Jindabyne. The rooms were kept neat and the bathroom was very clean. Stayed in the Deluxe Snow Gum Cabin which has a Queen sized bed plus a bunk bed (double at the bottom single on the top). The bathroom was a bit smaller than we expected but it was just what we needed. There was also a mini kitchen counter and an AC. The location was perfect as it was only a 30 min drive from Mt Kosciuszko and a 3 min drive to a Woolies and nearby restaurants.

Had a powered site. Great location with excellent and clean amenities. The lady on reception was very friendly and helpful and when we found our site was too windy for our camper, she let us switch to another site without any fuss whatsoever.  Would stay agqin.

Best place we have stayed to date! The ammenities are incredible, awesome camp kitchen with everything you could dream of, movie screen outside with movies each night, live music and they offered to cook our pizza for us on Saturday night. Everything was always clean and they even had bath mats for the shower rooms! Such an elite well thought out park. I would drive 8 hours each way everytime just to come back here
This is a lovely maintained caravan park, positioned right beside the lake. Great walks around the lake and off leash areas for dogs. Absolutely loved our week here!

The location is just amazing and much better than the other camp sites around.

The facilities though are somewhat dated and need refurbishment
refurbishment.

Also beware the ground is really hard and unless you got an electric drill you will struggle. We are camping the old-fashioned way with a tent and we damaged loads of steel pegs as we couldn't physically hammered them down.

We booked unpowered campsite, location was beautiful, sites were in front of lake with amazing views.
Amenities were clean, kids love the reading room and TV room.
The only drawback for us was amenities were very far from our sites.

Staff was welcoming and nice,

We will definitely comeback,

Great location right on Lake Jindabyne, close to walking trails, shopping centre, pubs and cafes. Also, Raul was operating a food van from the Holiday Park serving freshly cooked asian food - absolutely fabulous food and very handy to grab a quick bite!
Lovely area, beautiful clean cabins,nice and spacious. Tea, coffee provided.
traumhaft!

Best camping park I've stayed at, very nice bathrooms (a welcome reprieve from the drop toilets deep into Koz park) and an excellent view of the lake. Would reccomend a camping ground a little further from the lake though, it gets windy and our tents suffered.
Great place to set up camp. Beautiful views of the lake. Very good services. Efficient washers and drivers. The staff were very helpful and friendly.
Great place with great facilities!
Ich war auf dem Caracanpark. Sehr freundlich und sehr sauber.

The two ladies at front reception are so nice and down to earth. They had me laughing from the moment I walked in. The park is in a great location, right on the lake, and across the road from Woolies and other shops (walking distance). We had a powered site and our little dog with us also. The sites are small and not very flat so you may need chocks to level your van. Yes, you are quite close to other campers, but that did not bother us. There are coded amenities, a kiddies playground, grass for the dog, a nice walking area along the river and a good camp kitchen - and lots more. The only downside is that the amenities are cleaned at 8.30/9am, which, if you want to sleep in, you might have to wait for a pee!  Five star service, thank you.
Sites are a little bit small and not everyone has a view of the lake, but amenities are very clean and modern. Plenty to keep kids occupied and only a short walk to town centre.
Loved my 5days there inthe powered caravan section

Fabulous, super clean, friendly caravan park with amazing views over Lake Jindabyne!

The cleaners at this park take great pride in keeping the facilities clean and hygienic, and the service of offering a white towel bathmat to use in the showers is a great touch.
Toller Platz, alles vorhanden und sehr sauber. Der erste CP, den ich erlebt habe, der Badematten für die Duschen bereit stellt 👍

NRMA Jindabyne
The camping ground was absolutely awesome! Perfectly set on the edge of Lake Jindabyne, it offers peaceful surrounds and stunning views — the ideal spot for afternoon nibbles by the water 😍.
The amenities are spotless (bonus points for the thoughtful bath mats!), and the camp kitchen is one of the best we’ve seen — complete with an indoor BBQ, pizza oven, fridge, sink, and utensils all within easy reach. The outdoor bench and table seating were a great touch too.
A huge shout-out to Naomi in the office and the rest of the crew for being so warm, welcoming, and full of fantastic local tips. Couldn’t ask for better hosts — nothing was ever too much trouble!
There are some council roadworks happening nearby at the moment, but don’t let that put you off — they’re only temporary. Once you’re settled in, you won’t even notice.
If you’re looking for a little slice of paradise in the Snowy Mountains, we highly recommend NRMA Jindabyne!

Beautiful scenery
Plenty of parking
Nice and spacious units

Highly recommend this place to stay.
We camped with a lake front view.
Amenities are clean and plenty.
Great central location

This holiday park is in an incredible location - scenic and convenient if you’re wanting to be in central Jindabyne. We camped for 4 nights and had a beautiful view of the lake. Keep in mind the lake front tent sites don’t have any shade and no picnic tables/benches. The amenities were very clean and well maintained. Hot showers with bath mats and flushing toilets were available. There were only two downsides to our stay. First; I think the tent sites are in need of a grass seeding. Possibly because of caravans parking on the grass, there are lots of dirt/dust patches. When it’s windy the dust goes everywhere. Second; there are over fifty unpowered sites at NRMA. There were about four tents and we were all side by side. With so little people, separate us out a bit! Other than that, I highly recommend.
Excellent stay, clean facilities and great service
Great location and nice campground! The amenities were really good! Pretty view right on the lake

Beautiful well maintained grounds. Stayed in the tiny house and it was fantastic. Great central location, full amenities and good layout. Only downside was the lounge area didn’t have enough room for all 4 of us to sit and watch a movie in comfort. Beautiful views of the lake and plenty for the kids to do.

We stayed in the new Glamtainer #2 and it was amazing!  Beautiful big windows that keep the heat in and wonderful views of the lake and mountains.  Park amenities are very well looked after…great camp kitchen with pizza oven, huge laundry 8 washers and 5 dryers…all in working order; great shower facilities - I appreciate a shower cubicle with hooks and ledges and they even supply a bath mat!  Thought that was brilliant.  Great location in beautiful Jindabyne…this is our 3rd stay here and we’ve always loved it.

Fantastic camping experience! Pristine location right on the water’s edge, could not be more conveniently located as it’s a hop skip and a jump away from the town centre. All the facilities were wonderfully convenient, clean and well maintained. But the best part, the part that will bring me back every time from now - the showers! Nothing better than after a slog of a day, with toes numb from the cold, making your way into the perpetually warm and humid toilet block and having a hot high pressure shower.

This is an AWESOME place to stay during the snow season & only 33km from Perisher. We bought our van down and the park is within walking distance to everything you need. The sites are a great size and the staff are very friendly & obliging to assist with any requests. We will be back for sure 👍👍
Fantastic location and friendly staff. Great facilities and always clean
Lovely place, clean and comfortable.

I warn all travellers to check your messages before arrival ! After a long drive I was lectured about not checking messages prior to arrival and I was some how at fault for delaying the “express checking” ? After the lecture she then turned to my son and began to belittle me in front of of him regarding not to be a typical guy who doesn’t read messages properly . Sooo after all that when we left early before my end date the same same lady was in the office ready to have a go at me for checking out of the cabins late basically after the 10 am time , and I informed her about my updated plans and there was no acknowledgment of what she said to me about late checkout time hitch was incorrect ( I did not mistake this to be humour - on both occasions - very obnoxious and apparently entitled lady in charge of a caravan park , I will definitely try the neighbours next time- and for the previous review the facilities are old and outdated

Naomi and the team at NRMNA Jindabyne Holiday Park are nothing short of stellar. My son and I stayed there on a ski trip last year in a new studio with ski storage. The rooms were clean and quiet, the location fantastic and the staff wonderful with great coffee at the reception. We booked again this year but I fractured my ankle and couldn't make the trip. We will definitely be back though!

Naomi and the team at Jindabyne Holiday Park are nothing short of stellar. My son and I stayed last year for a ski holiday and loved it - the cabin was new, spotless, quiet and had a storage room for our ski gear. The reception had great coffee and excellent tips for the local area. We booked again this year and murphy's law - I fractured my ankle playing soccer and had to cancel the trip. Their customer service is second to none, giving helpful advice and all the time I needed to make the decision to do the trip or not with a fractured foot. A full refund was given for this trip but we will definitely be back. Thanks for your kindness, understanding and humanity - shame the same can't be said about EPIC Pass who are still arguing against a refund.
A top veiw of the lake , nice paths for awalk or run . Friendly staff , showers nice and hot . Lots a quite roomy .
Great location, easy access to Perisher.  We were given an upgrade so we had more room,  which was terrific.   Cabin was comfortable,  and well appointed.  Staff were very friendly.

Luke from Jindabyne caravans is amazing. Found pod warm and toasty with diesal heater on. Very comfy mattress.
Location perfect...walking distance to shops, cafes.

Has to be one of the better caravan parks in NSW.

Craig and Naomi are amazing park managers, their communication is ideal, and they are just great people. Also - make sure you pat Ace and Bob!

The park always has activities in winter and summer, such as Pizza Night or the Easter bunny visiting.

Amenities are well look after, shower pressure is great and warm in winter.

We stayed at kosi cabin for the long weekend  location is perfect  service is great but cabins definitely need upgrade  sliding doors doesn't work properly  floors clean mattress  definitely needs upgrade on main bedroom

We stayed in a powered site in a very convenient site which was close to all the town eateries and shops. The amenities were in very good condition however the bathroom entry (code based) system failed in the morning across all amenities. The management of this glitch seemed very inefficient with guests across the park unable to gain entry. It was inconvenient because it was from first thing in the morning and the flustered team was unable to pivot to a back up system (choking open the doors would have been the most simple and low tech way to manage it). Campers without onboard toilet/ensuite could not gain entry which was poor.

The view of the lake from our powered site was lovely.

Great position, close to shops and right on the lake. Good amenities. Unfortunately we got Covid the day after our arrival so I didn't leave the caravan but they were very happy to extend our stay on short notice so we could isolate and recover.

We stopped on a powered site lake front
Fantastic caravan park right in town.
Jindabyne is a beautiful town ❤️
We chose this place for its proximity to Kosciuszko National Park, but the lake views won us over too. Great spot close to town with Jandy facilities and friendly staff.

NRMA Jindabyne Holiday Park is a fantastic spot near Lake Jindabyne, perfect for families and outdoor lovers. Naomi and Shane were the real MVPs (give them a raise), offering helpful local tips that made our stay even better. The park features a scenic bike and walking track around the lake leading to Sakete Park, a hit with the kids. We stayed in the cozy Kosi Cabin, ideal for four, with modern amenities and strong shower water pressure-though a few spider webs could use a quick clean. With clean facilities, playgrounds, and a great location close to nature and town, this park is a top choice for a fun, relaxing getaway.

14/4/2025-17/04/2025

We stayed at an unpowered site and really enjoyed it — the spacing between sites didn’t feel too cramped, and we were lucky to have a front-row lake view, which was absolutely beautiful.
The overall facilities seemed well-maintained, and it felt like there was regular upkeep, rather than being left unclean for long periods.

The water pressure in the showers wasn’t particularly strong, but it was still within an acceptable range.

The only major downside was the number of bees — there were quite a lot around the campsite. My partner got stung, though thankfully there was no allergic reaction.

I did not enjoy my stay this Easter! The ammenities are very dated & dirty. There are not a lot of facilities for older children compared to other NRMA parks. There is a sever wasp problem. My son is allergic to wasps & was too scared to go outside. Couldn’t even leave our camper door open, eat or cook outside as they were everywhere. I do understand that this is out of your control but it was a huge issue. We had items stolen on one of the nights we were there also. It is not a secure park & has a lot of access to general  public. Our camp site was uneven, had a  hole in it & was very cramped.
We will not be staying again!
Best camping site in the area. Nice and clean amenities.

Amazing customer service, all staff are super friendly and welcoming.
Nice clean cabins and amenities.

Situated in the heart of the town, perfect views of the beautiful lake, a few minutes walk to the local shops.

The ladies at reception do a wonderful job and are always happy answer any questions.

Can’t wait to return!

We really enjoyed our few days at Jindabyne Holiday Park. It was quiet and walking distance to restaurants and shops.

There is a great bike riding/walking track from the park to Hatchery Bay.

The amenities were spotless. Highly recommend!

We spent 4 nights in our caravan on site A43. Initially I was hoping for a lake view site but it was extremely hot while we were staying at the park and we were very grateful for both the protection from the wind and the beautiful poplar trees which helped to keep us cool.
Communication was excellent, even receiving a text message with a weather warning for the next day.
Sites were a good size and easy to get our van onto, good amenities, nice clean hot showers and an easy walk across the road if you want a coffee or groceries.

I booked the only cabin left, Brumby, without a bathroom. I really enjoyed my 3 night stay. The cabin and facilities were clean and well stocked. The mattress was so comfortable. Well located & quiet. Would stay again.
Perfect budget friendly and amazing location

To start off with I was told I had paid for the holiday in full and was told I need to pay another $10 on the day via a phone call as we were driving on a major highway with nowhere to pull over, when I asked why I want given a reason. We weren't provided any guidance on how to checkin as we were arriving at 6:30pm and reception was closed. When we did make it to our cabin there was dirt all over the floor and our bed. On checkout through our own fault as I thought I booked an extra night we were called at 11:45 when checkout was at 10. The phone call came across a little bit rude and we were asked to do dishes and take rubbish out before we leave. It would've been nice if we were sent a kind text the night before reminding us kindly about checkout the next day to save everyone the inconvenience.

Very Quiet park close to the lake and town
Very neat and tidy amenities and very good showers .
The Staff where so friendly and helpful and nothing seemed to much trouble sadly the manager could take a leaf out of there book as she  is very abrupt and quiet rude both on the phone and in person.
Still a very nice place to stay

We rented a Glamtainer last winter August 2024 ( just found my review drafted in my notes!)

Checkin was easy. Management were very friendly and helpful.

We had GT01 during winter. We had good weather though if raining the deck would be unusable without a roof, though the view is lovely. The deck could benefit from a little gate to keep kiddies in as the 2nd bedroom is well acquainted as a kids bedroom.
The glass sliding door quality had me amazed, I wish ours at home was so good! The insulation and sound proofing from outside is great, however the air con noise is noticeable at times like the dull drone of a semi trailer truck idling outside. The air con makes the metal kitchen cupboards rattle.
The temperature outside all day was maximum of 10 degrees however we came back to the container being 35+ degrees inside - the air con worked well to bring it back down to a normal temperature.

The quality of furnishings is very good, however the click lock flooring in parts was cracked and a bit sharp. A few things were broken and addressed with management.

It was a good experience and i'd recommend it highly to anyone contemplating buying a container home or just wants a nice stylish accomm with a good view.

We would stay here again if the price was cheaper but it was a splurge for us.

Bad service - understand there are late check-ins - but the rudeness of the lady over the phone 📞 - just for that - give it a miss . I'm actually a member of the NRMA - pity
The caravan park was half empty ! !
Even if I did on-line booking - what difference does it make if we get rejected over the phone 📞 ?
Some parks have a find a spot - fix us in the morning routine .
Great place to stay ,but very busy and lots of people ,easy walk to lake good showers and toilets ,easy walk to bakery and woollies,great views if you book eary and get front row

$50 p/night for an uneven, un-powered site, with no lake views and wifi black spot, is totally unreasonable.
I know this is Jindabyne, but $50 is way too much!
The amenities are clean and usable.
Reception was very good and helpful.
The location to shops, restaurants, and town is fantastic.
The most wonderful memories from the two nights there. Clean, spacious amenities, quiet place, you can have a great swim in the lake after conquering Mt Kosciuszko. Amazing!
Rude staff

Der Platz ist okay. Die Caravans stehen mit gutem Abstand, es hat jeder seine Privatsphäre. Die Landry lässt allerdings Wünsche offen. Auf die Größe des Platzes gibt es eindeutig zuwenig Waschmaschinen. Ärgerlich ist der Anteil der Maschinen mit warmen oder heißen Wasser. Die Duschen sind in Ordnung allerdings sollten die Wasserhähne festgestellt werden, es ist nicht schön wenn man die beim Duschen in der Hand hält und wieder anbasteln muss. |Das Personal ist sehr freundlich und hilfsbereit. Alles im Allem ist es ein guter Platz und weiterzuempfehlen.

This is a well organised park, offering a range of levels of accommodation, EV charging, access to the lake, close to shops. My only gripe is that access to the shops, while nearby, involves crossing a rather dangerous section of the main road. Not the parks fault, but there should be a safe crossing between the lake and the shops. This is a great park in a top location and is a great base for adventures in the Snowies.
Friendly staff, clean amenities, close to shops 🤗

While we generally enjoy our experience at NRMA Jindabyne, I must highlight a significant issue with customer service. Unfortunately, whenever I call and speak with a woman named Chantelle, it becomes the worst experience! She keeps repeating the same thing over and over and over and overe again without offering any real assistance or useful solutions. Furthermore, her demeanor is short, arrogant, and rude, which only adds to the frustration. It's disappointing to encounter such unhelpful service in an otherwise pleasant environment.
Always my first choice for accommodation. Right in front of the lake. Best for walking on  the lakeside. BBQ and kitchen and other facilities available.

For what you charge internet service should be strong and uninterrupted. Please consider changing to Starlink -no excuse its nearly 2025.
Grounds can be tidier and bathroom snd toilet facilities need to be cleaned more frequently.
Very nice camping !! Naomi and Craig are wonderful and so funny. Thank’s so much for the beautiful location, point view and Christmas pancake 🥞 ❤️🎄
Great place to stay at Jindabyne. Right on the lake and can walk into town
Great spot. Central to the NSW Park, shops, etc.
Not very nice customer service.
Camped by the water!

Very nice holiday park right on the edge of Lake Jindabyne. Amenities, BBQs very clean and a really nice place to stay while exploring Snowy Mountains, Thredbo, Charlotte Pass and Perisher. Very nice and helpful lady on reception. Thank you
Beautiful location by the lake, good and clean amenities and nice staff. We can just recommend staying here while visiting Jindabyne.
Nice new cabin, walking distance to shops, restaurants and pub.
Great customer service and very well maintained park. Will definitely stay again, also very dog friendly.

Location, location, location!
We camped down by the lake and thoroughly enjoyed our stop here. It is a convenient place to stop while exploring the surrounding area.
The staff were friendly and the facilities were clean. The park is also within walking distance of restaurants and shops.

Stayed there on the weekend of 30th Aug to 2nd Sept. There were strong winds blowing in sand from the shore banks of the lake through the window into the Master Bedroom. The whole room was filled with a layer of sand on the bed and floor. When I reported this, all I got was a "You're lucky you're not one of those camping outside"

I requested a cabin transfer and there was none available.
Friendly staff and quiet spot to camp
Great location and price
Always nice to stay here, but new managers are definatly not as engaging as the previous manager. It most likely takes time to get to know the guest like family
We stayed here because of the location. The room was ok... but the cabin's floor was dusty and some pots and pans were dirty.

Definitely more on the expensive side of things for a unpowered site. It's obviously super popular, but I could imagine this place could be mayhem on weekends and even worse school holidays. The view is definitely nice though

We recently stayed here for a few nights and I was unhappy with the state of our cabin. It wasn't cleaned properly, our linen wasn't cleaned properly and it had tiny spiders in it and our beds were unmade on arrival. Our toilet flusher was broken so the toilet kept running. Our stove top wasn't working properly so we weren't able to cook anything. The bbq outside didn't stay on long enough to cook anything so we had to keep relighting it and it ended up taking 2 hours to cook our food. The games was disappointing, the pool table was missing balls and there was only one pool cue so we wasted our money even trying to play. It's in a lovely location, but alot of maintenance needs to be done and the cleaning staff need to be more thorough, for the price we paid I was very disappointed.
Well situated park close to shops and lake
Great place to wake up and explore Jindabyne. Pizza Saturdays with live music is a regular occurrence 👌🙏💯
Good caravan camping site.
Maureen seriously needs to work on her customer service skills cannot believe how rude she was.
就是贵了点

Great place to stay if you're not fussy. The Brumby cabins are small but beds are ok and they are air conditioned. Small basic kitchen. No bathroom in these so use the facilities block which is pretty good. Best part is location, walk to town across the road, enjoy the lakeside position.
As usual NRMA resorts did not disappoint me, looks perfect

My son left his Nintendo there I paid to get it sent back and still haven't received it ? When I call and ask the staff they say they dont know where it is or who or if they sent it back when I notified them that I sent a envelope out to them. I'm being left in the dark.

Very beautiful place and has an amazing view of the lake. Location is perfect is accesible and has close proximity to shops and with just a few mins drive to Snowy Mountains. Place has everything you need. Would definitely come back.

It was really amazing experience! Great location near the lake and  friendly cheerful staff! We enjoyed a pizza night, my special thanx to Naomi for looking after us! Deffinitely recommend as a winter holiday destination :-)  Nature here is awesome too by the way

Today I called to ensure that my campsite number was still the same, as last year, my reserved campsite was moved to another one arbitrarily before my arrival.

This year, before I made the payment, the staff assured me that my reserved campsite would not be changed. However, when I called to confirm the reservation before arrival, the staff said that they weren't sure  we would get our reserved campsite due to some potential or unexpected emergency situations, such as 'casualties' or 'illnesses', which confused me because they didn't tell me those conditions before.

When I tried to enquire more about the different situations, the customer service staff became impatient, repeatedly interrupted my inquiries and hung up the call.

I felt deeply disappointed with the Jindabyne Holiday Park customer service. I think that if they need to change our specific campsite booking due to their business needs during busy seasons, they should explain the conditions in detail before we make a payment. However, if we have reserved a specific campsite based on an official online map and they already confirmed that our campsite wouldn't be changed, they should not arbitrarily change our reservation on our arrival.

The most terrible customer service I had ever experienced.
Really disgusting uneven caravan sites. Bathrooms weren’t washed entire time and very unprofessional staff. Do not go here!!

We rented four caravan-powered sites. Although they did not have a lake view, this wasn't an issue since we have toddlers. The sites were conveniently close to the very clean toilets and kitchen. The amenities included clean facilities, a kids' playground, warm showers, and laundry facilities. Across the street from the campsite, about an eight-minute walk away, there are many food places and grocery stores. You can also rent snow skis at the row house. Remember to take the discount card from the reception. Additionally, there is a bus service that avoids the expensive ski tube to Perisher.
Not the best when I entered the room there was like a curry smell

Excellent place for stay, beautiful lake view ( best from Lake View Villa), BBQ, Camping close to Perisher Valley.
It was a lovely stay for us as we expected.

Was an amazing night with families.
Very decent and helpful staff at reception.
Must recommend.
Cheers
Perfect location with reasonable prices. Everything is good with their service.

I am blown away by how lovely the studios are here, amazing value, and the best hot shower I’ve had in a long time! Ladies at the front desk were welcoming and friendly, and a perfect location to explore Jindabyne. I’ll be recommending this spot to everyone :)

Powered Van site , Great caravan park ,amenities and staff , Camp kitchen was well fitted out and amenities are very clean , We stayed the second week in April and be aware it was extremely cold , Across the road from Jindabyne shops which was handy, Great outlook across Lake Jindabyne, Our only gripe was the site we were allocated was very tight and under a tree that had a lot of birds and there droppings , Really is a great spot
Located on the shores of Lake Jindabyne, it's good to see EV Charging facilities onsite, even if I use a petrol car myself.
Great caravan park with a wonderful view over the lake. So.e of the facilities are starting to show their age.

Craig and Naomi have done an excellent job in a short time frame! The level of communication across our weekend stay was excellent from easy check-in, to what’s on updates and suggestions about town. Would recommend this stay to families and travellers.
Cleanliness of amenities and laundry was great with hot showers at all times.
Our campsite was well maintained and they even allowed us a few of our extra requests even on a busy weekend. Very accommodating and we will be back!
Would love to see a cashless (card operated) addition to the laundry however this didn’t hamper us at all it’s just a nicety.
From all the crew at Here4Beer.4x4 on a big trip we can’t thank you enough!

Had a brilliant 9 days here. Superb caravan site in an amazing location at a good price. Easy access to the lake and close to town. Woolworths etc and a public pool a short walk over the road. It has all the facilities you need and it is very clean especially in the kitchen and bathrooms. TV room, reading room, pool table, laundry, tennis, bike wash, play area, pizza oven, bbq’s, electric bike hire, EV car charging. Disc golf a short walk away.

Schön am Jindabyne See gelegen. Die Sites sind zum Teil etwas schief, aber wir konnten uns mit einem Brett helfen. Amenities sauber und in Ordnung. Ruhige Lage. Prima Joggingstrecke gleich am See, gut ausgestattete Camp Kitchen, sogar mit Spüli und sauberen Handtüchern. Einkaufszentrum mit Woolworths und Restaurants zu Fuß erreichbar, gleich in der Nähe.

We have been coming to Jindabyne for the past 7 years same time every year.|I absolutely love the place and its handy location to the mountains and shops.|The only negative i would give this park is every year we are on a lake view site near the boat ramp.|These sites are premium sites, why do they put caravans on the unpowered sites blocking the view of people who pay premium dollar for these sites.|The unpowered sites oposite the powered sites should be  be for tents or small campers.|Kirily and Freddy were great managers and the new managers will have big shoes to fill and i do wish them well.|Feb 2024 was our last year at this time.|Amenties are spotless and the park is kept extremely neat and tidy.|

What a great place along the Jindabyne Lake for summer holiday. We stayed 3nights in Kosi Cabin. It had all amenities you needed for. Park is also filled with huge Cottonwood trees that are turning yellow now. It will be so beautiful in Autumn.

Con: AC will not support for hot days even if you keep it on for whole day.
Great for what it is. Clean, all the mod cons, and parking right next to each cabin.
Friendly helpful staff,  the grounds need lots of love,  however there is some action.
They should stick to selling insurance. Tiny sites, amenities need a refurb or at least a good gurni
We had a great camp site Jindabyne is a very nice spot in summer  the lake is a great spot the NRMA Caravan  park is recommended

Jindabyne is a stunning place with amazing shops and cafes and a gorgeous lake to swim in. The holiday park was also really nice with great amenities and services, the water was turned off for a few hours so we couldn’t use them but it was sorted out quite quickly.

Our only grievance was that our camp site was 50% slope, this meant we didn’t really have any space around our car to set up our table, while other sites had tons of free space, we were crammed between our car and the car on the site next to us. It wasn’t terrible but also not ideal.

Overall we would definitely recommend this park.

This is my fav place to camp. It has all the facilities you need, gorgeous weather during the hot summer months in other locations. I can camp right on the lake front and work from my computer in the most gorgeous 😍 location.

The staff are always super friendly and the other campers here are usually always so nice and helpful.

I love coming here and return regularly when QLD is too hot.
beautiful spot

We stayed on a unpowered-site with our van. Location is great, unpowered-sites by the lake offers a wonderful view of the lake and mountains. Staff were nice and helpful. Very clean and well maintained caravan park. We will come back.

Stayed in a powered site. Loved the provided site mat. The site was a good size for our 19ft van.
Didn't use any of the amenities this trip.
Staff communicated frequently with updates via sms.

Better now than snowline. More room boat parking. Only annoying thing is access to facilities cuts out at 10am day of leaving. They should cut this off at 12pm to allow people to use facilities they have paid for prior to leaving. We vacated sire at 10am but then had to get new code from office before we drove out etc. Hope the place reads this and foes adjust it as people travelling often use families before leaving. Waste of time for reception staff too.
Good view, well-maintained amenities
Basic cabins but unbeatable location on the lake.
Campground was completely flooded and service was poor

Small powered sites. Nice area. Not much to do if you don’t ski hike or bike. Managers were nice, some permanent onsite vans as in most parks. The lake is quite low but nice sunsets to be seen. Roadworks being undertaken in park.
Very well maintained, excellent facilities. We camped on a shore-side site. Worth staying.
Quiet nice, tent side just at the lake. Prices are an offence, increased by 100% from one day to the other without a reason - those businesses should die
WiFi doesn't work

A great place where you can leave your car parked and just walk to the shops and lake. Very friendly staff and beautiful flowers everywhere. We stayed in two of the Glamtainers which were perfect after 4 days and nights roughing it in the National Park. Very peaceful. Love the rabbits munching on the green grass! The Herb garden near the camp kitchen has everything you need to add some zing to your meal. Thanks for a great stay from four Queenslanders.
Love olace,friendly staff. Great rates for NRMA members

You can't really fault the location of this place. Well positioned right on the Lakeshore, great views and close to all amenities. I feel like the actual cabin accommodation is a bit underwhelming and overpriced. It's ok, but you are definitely getting gouged on the price simply because of where it is.

Sites are not expensive and the location is perfect, in front of the lake and 5min walk from the shopping centre.
Amenities are very clean and tidy, there is a reading room and a tv room, a tennis court, a big kitchen... Staff is friendly and welcoming,
There are some cute rabbits during the night around the lake 🥰
One of our favourite camping in Australia 🦘

Worried that I've just read a review from 2022 stating that he mattresses were badly stained. We stayed very end of October in a Lake View lodge. The single bed/ bunk mattresses are not fit for a dog to sleep on. See photo unbelievably stained and ripped. On reporting this to reception we were told that there were no replacement mattresses available and new ones had been ordered..I wonder whether that is at all true?|Also we question whether the mattress protectors are changed after each visit as the staff do not make the beds up because of the awkward angles.|The lodges are old and tired but functionable however asking paying guests to sleep on disgusting beds is dreadful.
Had a side trip to Jindabyne on our way to Melbourne from Sydney. Spent one night. The cottage was enough for us five. It was located near the lakeside.

Great caravan park , well laid out . Well she’d grounds with good size sites .|Excellent showers ,super hot water and very clean .camp kitchen was large and very clean with a great layout . Lake side sites with perfect views for that sunrise or sunset
Great park. Lots of trip hazards. Sticks ,rocks,stones uneven ground. Be careful. Very clean and spacious amenities.
We have been staying there with our caravan during winter as we love skiing. The facilities are fantastic well maintained and super clean.
Always a great time

The website stated a pet friendly park park. It is not. I was told after arriving that in winter no dogs this is despite the fact that I arrived 11 September at 2pm at 4:30pm I was told I had to find other accommodation for my dog who was tethered the entire (2.5 hours) that I was there, while other dogs roamed around. I wasn’t even given a nights grace. Absolutely appalling to do this to a single mum with 2 kids 8 hours away from home.

Called in to get a cabin for the night and parked in front of the reception because I am disabled and the woman came out screaming at me and telling me to move it. So I moved the car and went in apologising for the mistake. She said they only had one cabin and it was $300. So I left and got a motel

My recent stay for my birthday was quite disappointing. The studio we booked was incredibly small, to the point where it was a struggle to take more than a few steps inside. The reception staff were far from friendly, and their directions to our studio were extremely unclear, leading us to waste at least 10 minutes driving up and down trying to find it. The bathroom in the studio was also cramped, and the shower was so tiny that I could barely stand comfortably. Overall, it was not the enjoyable birthday getaway we had hoped for.

Called in to get a cabin for the night and being tired parked on the wrong side as that was where reception was. The elderly lady came out and yelled abuse at me to move it, which I did. When I went in I was polite to her and she said she only had one cabin for $300 for the night. I said no thankyou and left. I am disabled and didn't really need to be treated like that. We found a beautiful motel for half the price.
It is looking like it needs some money spent. I feel that the owners have no interest in improving the place.
Unreal location!!! Easy check in process and staff really helpfully :) great park Allround for anyone!! Great for groups!!!

Great service front desk lady was nice and helpful, abit dissapointing when it’s -1 degrees and u have a secure fire pit and on a calm night, tells us to put fire out pretty easy to control a small fire. park manager was disrespectful and abrupt about having a small fire other then that it’s a great spot and good front desk lady just disappointed with no fires when your finger tips are falling off and your camping in a small tent..
Perfect location on the lake. Not all camp sites have power which is pretty standard. Reasonably flat sites. Amenities are very good. Also has a communal wood fire pizza oven

Apart from the park manager and location, the place is old, tired and everything is falling apart. The level of cleanliness on room make up was a joke.
We rented a snow gum cabin for 4 nights. Toilet was leaking, kitchen was oily and no one has bothered cleaning it properly for a year it seems.
Mattress was the most uncomfortable and had a slope on it, so one of us always woke up with a sore back.
Over all paying $550 A night in peak season , one still expects a better living experience. And did I mention Wi-Fi? Never worked when we needed it in our 4 nights stay there....
It was my first and last time ever to stay at this NRMA facility....
8 ball pool games and fire

The receptionist was efficient  but not friendly or welcoming.
The location was great but the sites tight for caravans.
Extremely happy with our stay, our cabin was clean, could not fault it.
I went there to look at a caravan for sale. Such helpful managers who showed me around. Beautiful location on the lake edge, very clean too.
We have stayed here 3 times now and always have had a wonderful experience

Great location on the lake and across from woolies. We stayed at a cabin. I do struggle with soft mattresses so I didn’t sleep as well. Temperature was great with the A/C inverter on. We enjoyed the pool table and the BBQ. Nice fire pit going on and on Saturday night they had the pizza oven on so people could bring their own pizza.
Love this park I generally stay in the studio cabins. Great location & run how a caravan tourist park should be. 5 stars.
